strengths: Strong SQL and Python + ETL experience
weaknesses: Limited explicit A/B testing/experiment design experience
Less than required 4+ years in analytics roles
Sparse evidence of responsible/ethical AI practices
improvement_bullets: Developed and executed A/B testing frameworks for marketing campaigns, utilizing hypothesis testing to measure user response and optimize conversion strategies for high-value segments.
Led end-to-end product analytics initiatives, defining success metrics and monitoring product health to drive roadmap prioritization and feature adoption.
Engineered advanced prompts and context-managed workflows using AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT) to automate data quality checks and ethical bias mitigation in model outputs.
Designed and monitored controlled experiments to validate product performance, applying statistical rigor to evaluate feature impact on key product metrics.
Partnered with cross-functional product and engineering teams to identify user behavioral trends, utilizing data-driven storytelling to influence product strategy and long-term ecosystem growth.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Data Scientist, Product Analytics opportunity with Meta. I am interested in this opportunity because of Meta’s unparalleled ability to connect billions of users through social technology and its commitment to using data to solve complex, real-world product challenges. What interests me about working for Meta is its mission to build immersive experiences that go beyond the constraints of screens; I am deeply inspired by the scale at which Meta’s product teams iterate and improve lives through data-driven innovation. I believe my expertise in analytical modeling and my proficiency in building robust ETL pipelines would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by translating complex data into actionable product strategy. In fact, at Shine Masters, I designed a time series model to identify high-value customer segments and, through iterative testing and segmentation, drove a 300% increase in new customer acquisition over 12 months. This experience directly translates to the product growth and monitoring responsibilities required at Meta. Ultimately, I know that the Data Scientist, Product Analytics role at Meta would allow me to bring my technical skill in SQL and Python to drive product development and roadmap decision-making. Additionally, my role as a Data Analytics Fellow at COOP Careers enabled me to develop my data storytelling skills. I led an analysis of 250K+ records using Python to identify demographic patterns and presented these insights to stakeholders through an interactive Tableau dashboard, resulting in clear recommendations for public safety initiatives. I am excited by the opportunity to use my background in experimental analysis and product-focused metrics in working on the family of applications at Meta. I know that my technical versatility and analytical rigor align with what it takes to succeed at the Data Scientist, Product Analytics role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
